restaurant.qrHint: Scan this QR code to open the restaurant page directly on a phone.

pairing.headline: Choose a dish. Choose a wine. Read the rationale below.
pairing.subheading: The screen is organized as two side-by-side lists. Dish selection updates the wine ranking, wine selection reorders the menu list, and the lower panel acts like a bot chat without text input.
pairing.explanationLine: The explanation is shown as a bot response based only on the selected records.
pairing.sommelierBotSubtitle: Automated rationale based on selected records
pairing.botFallbackReason: This wine sits outside the top match zone for the selected dish, but the style can still be reviewed through the passport and ranking signals.

noData.title: No pairing data available
noData.subtitle: Add dishes and wines from the new admin panel, then return to the pairing screen.

pitch.metaDescription: A sommelier in every guest's pocket. Cellar Compass turns your menu and cellar into a quiet conversation with every diner.

hero.title: A sommelier
hero.lede: Cellar Compass turns your menu and cellar into a quiet conversation with every guest. No staff training. No backend. Live in five minutes.
hero.footnote: This is a working prototype. A pilot programme is the next step.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Four moments — from a printed QR to a poured glass.
how.step1Body: Each venue gets a unique URL and QR. Print, laminate, slip into the bill folder. The diner scans, the experience opens — no app to install.
how.step2Body: The menu is yours, with photos and prices. Choosing a dish is the only input — there is no chat box, no tool to learn.
how.step3Body: An algorithm tuned to body, acidity, and tannin scores every label on your list. The top match is auto-selected with the rationale shown like a quiet message from the bot.
how.step4Body: Hand-curated overrides take precedence. A sentence written by your sommelier — in English and Polish — replaces the algorithm's draft.

what.title: What you get
what.subtitle: A short list. Each item earns its place.
what.f1Body: Direct URL and printable code for each location.
what.f2Body: Real-world Leaflet+OSM. Guests find you, agencies find your network.
what.f3Body: Pick a dish — wines re-order. Pick a wine — the menu re-orders.
what.f4Body: Your wording, not the machine's. Curated entries beat the algorithm.
what.f5Body: Every dish, every wine, every rationale — in both languages.
what.f6Body: Designed for the phone in the diner's hand, not the manager's laptop.
what.f7Body: All edits live in the browser. Export JSON to move venues between devices.
what.f8Body: Open the admin, paste your menu, generate the QR. Done.

screenshots.title: Three views, one product
screenshots.subtitle: What the guest sees, what you see, what your sommelier edits.
screenshots.phoneSub: A bot reply, no input box

pricing.title: Engagement
pricing.subtitle: We start small, on purpose. The demo is honest about being a demo.
pricing.tier1Tagline: What you are looking at right now.
pricing.tier2Tagline: One restaurant, three weeks, one verdict.

faq.title: Questions a restaurant owner asks
faq.a1: You do. The demo keeps everything in your browser; the production tier writes to a database under your name with a clean export at any time.
faq.q2: Can we export and walk away?
faq.a2: Yes. The admin has Export JSON today. Production adds CSV and a one-click backup to your Google Drive or S3 bucket.
faq.q3: What about the wine photos and prices?
faq.a3: The demo carries placeholder labels. Before any guest-facing pitch we replace each entry with a verified vintage photo and current market price — that is part of the pilot.
faq.a4: Yes — only to open the page. Once loaded the experience is offline-capable; we cache the menu and pairings via the service worker.
faq.a5: EN and PL are live now. Adding Italian, French, Spanish or German is a one-day swap of the locale dictionary; the schema is already shaped for it.
faq.q6: What happens after a year of demo?
faq.a6: Either we have run a paid pilot together, or you walk away with the JSON export and your QR codes in your pocket. There is no lock-in by design.

finalCta.title: Open the demo for your sommelier
finalCta.subtitle: Two minutes. No login. Print a QR before the next service.
